mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales growth signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue momentum for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the RSAFS trend is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value was 357013 as of one month before 2005-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 3% after a rapid six-month increase raises equity discount rates and tightens financing conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.0% and rises more than 75 bp over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 3.26 as of one month before 2005-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with low unemployment signals durable income growth without an imminent recession, raising earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE is at or below 5.2%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 5.0 as of one month before 2005-08-15
regime_hint: expansion with tight but not deteriorating labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-low signal has a 20-day hit rate below 52% and a Rank IC below 0.03 across two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Very high housing starts during a rising-rate cycle signal late-cycle interest-sensitive demand vulnerability, increasing the risk of future construction slowdown and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is above 1900 and FEDFUNDS rises more than 75 bp over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value was 2004 as of two months before 2005-08-15
regime_hint: late-cycle housing boom under monetary tightening
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if drawdown frequency is not higher than in untriggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production crossing into stronger short-run growth signals improving real output and operating leverage, supporting earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value was 119.657 as of two months before 2005-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO growth-crossing signals have average 20-day S&P 500 returns below zero or fail to exceed unconditional returns in at least 55% of walk-forward years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI while the federal funds rate is already rising signals restrictive policy risk and margin pressure from inflation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation crosses above its prior 12-month average and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value was 194.1 as of two months before 2005-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI-acceleration and FEDFUNDS-rising signal is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or has nonnegative average excess return.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and weaker future income growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 5.0 as of one month before 2005-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE deteriorations do not precede lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than nontriggered periods or if the triggered hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly CPI increase triggers near-term inflation concern and higher expected policy rates, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if CPI spike triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if the average 5-day return is not below zero.
